name: Improve solver3 construction relative broad-suite score

description: Baseline for softened relative broad-suite construction metric
Repeat baseline measurement for relative metric noise calibration
Increase outside-region move penalty under relative metric
Tried stronger selected-region move bonus with outside penalty; worsened relative score despite better 169x13 constrained

asi.hypothesis: Increasing the within-selected-region move bonus might preserve selected-template basin locality while the existing outside-region penalty prevents disruptive pulls.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary broad_relative_score regressed versus current best; the improvement on score_sgp_169x13x14_constrained was outweighed by worse Sailing, large immovable, clique, 49x7 constrained, and a zero-regression penalty.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not simply increase selected-region move bonus globally. Prefer more targeted merge scoring that reduces outside pulls without over-prioritizing any selected-region move.

description: Tried lower scaffold-disruption weight under relative metric; near best but still worse and adds zero-regression

asi.hypothesis: Under the relative metric, a lower scaffold-disruption penalty may let the oracle improve large cases such as Sailing enough to beat the old log-optimized 3x setting.
asi.rollback_reason: The relative mean improved, but one zero-baseline regression penalty made the primary metric slightly worse than current best; several constrained SGP sentinels also regressed.
asi.next_action_hint: A more selective zero-regression guard or template choice may be needed; avoid globally lowering disruption weight without protecting zero-baseline solved cases.

description: Tried 2.5x scaffold-disruption weight under relative metric; regressed broadly

asi.hypothesis: A moderate scaffold-disruption weight may capture part of the 2x large-case benefit while avoiding the zero-regression/balance issues.
asi.rollback_reason: 2.5x did not preserve the 2x benefits and regressed the relative mean plus zero-regression guard; Sailing, large immovable, Google CP, and constrained SGP signals worsened.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not tune disruption weight further in this simple scalar form unless paired with a principled zero-regression or candidate-risk mechanism.

description: Tried stronger current-group keep bonus with outside penalty; regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A stronger bonus for keeping a person in its scaffold group when that group is also the oracle target could preserve basins while still accepting clear oracle-aligned placements.
asi.rollback_reason: The primary relative metric worsened; zero-regression penalty appeared and large immovable / synthetic / 49x7 constrained sentinels regressed despite some constrained 169x13 improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally raise keep_bonus. If preserving current groups is needed, make it conditional on objective/risk signals rather than a larger blanket constant.

description: Tried stronger outside-region move penalty 3x; over-constrained merge and regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A stronger outside-region move penalty might further avoid basin-hostile pulls while preserving the useful 2x direction.
asi.rollback_reason: 3x over-constrained the merge: relative mean worsened, zero-regression penalty returned, and large/SGP constrained sentinels regressed versus the 2x keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep outside-region penalty at 2.0; do not tune it upward as a scalar.

description: Revert outside-region penalty to 1.0 under relative metric

asi.hypothesis: Under the relative metric, the original outside-region penalty may be preferable to the old kept 2.0 value because large real-world improvements now matter more.
asi.learned: Reverting outside-region penalty to 1.0 improved the primary relative metric below 1.0 with no zero-regression penalty, mainly through a much better Sailing score while preserving no-template sentinels.
asi.next_action_hint: Stop scalar-only tuning and pursue larger heuristic changes such as oracle-guided baseline fill or multi-template/risk-aware candidate choice.

description: Tried surgical oracle merge instead of clearing template groups; regressed relative score

asi.hypothesis: A surgical merge that moves accepted projected people and displaces only target-group occupants as needed should preserve more of the baseline search basin than clearing every selected template group.
asi.rollback_reason: The structural change did preserve Sailing reasonably but regressed the primary relative metric through zero-regression penalty and worse large constrained SGP, especially 169x13x14 constrained.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not replace clear-and-repair with this naive surgical merge. If revisiting surgical merge, add stronger capacity/zero-regression risk selection or candidate choice first.

description: Tried projection-aware top-3 oracle template selection; caused two benchmark failures

asi.hypothesis: Choosing among the top three templates using projection-aware score should avoid bad top-ranked templates without score-gating final acceptance.
asi.rollback_reason: The naive projection-aware top-3 selector caused two benchmark case failures (large gender immovable and transfer attribute sentinels became failure placeholders), so the candidate selector was not strict/safe enough.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not evaluate/choose multiple templates solely by projection score. A future multi-template path needs hard feasibility/risk prechecks or failure isolation without masking strict merge errors.

description: Return optimal warmup scaffold before oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: If the full-objective warmup scaffold already has zero score, oracle injection can only preserve or worsen it, so returning the scaffold before template generation is a generic optimal-incumbent guard rather than a fallback.
asi.learned: The zero-score scaffold guard slightly improved the relative metric and kept zero-regression count at 0. It mainly protects solved cases while preserving useful oracle behavior elsewhere.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue structural work, but keep this optimal-scaffold guard. Next focus on oracle-guided fill/risk selection for nonzero scaffolds.

description: Tried accepting oracle moves only when target beats current group; damaged SGP structure badly

asi.hypothesis: Accepted oracle placements should be advisory: only move a person if the oracle target scores better than staying in its current scaffold group under local pair/placement signals.
asi.rollback_reason: The local stay-vs-target filter broke the pure/SGP oracle structure badly, introducing multiple zero-baseline regressions and a huge 169x13 constrained regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not filter individual oracle moves independently against current-group score; preserving coherent whole-template structure matters for SGP cases.

description: Tried two-second warmup scaffold budget; caused a benchmark failure despite better relative mean

asi.hypothesis: A longer warmup search may produce a better full-objective scaffold before oracle injection, improving final construction+search quality despite less search time after construction.
asi.rollback_reason: The two-second warmup improved the non-penalized relative mean but introduced one benchmark failure and much higher construction time, making the primary metric catastrophic.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally increase warmup to 2s. A future adaptive warmup needs feasibility safeguards and should not consume budget blindly.

description: Repeat current zero-score scaffold guard line under relative metric

asi.hypothesis: Repeat the current kept structural line to estimate noise and confirm the zero-score scaffold guard remains beneficial under the relative metric.
asi.learned: The same code can vary materially, but this repeat stayed below 1.0 with no failures or zero-regressions; the guard remains plausible and noise is large enough that future keeps should preferably be structural and robust across sentinel tradeoffs.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with structural changes, not scalar tuning; compare sentinel mix carefully because Sailing and SGP constrained scores are noisy.

description: Tried cohort-aware displaced repair scoring in oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: When oracle merge displaces people, repair should fill slots using scaffold cohort agreement in addition to placement frequency so the incumbent stays search-basin-friendly rather than arbitrary.
asi.rollback_reason: Cohort-aware repair worsened the primary metric, especially the Sailing sentinel, while not improving constrained SGP enough to offset it.
asi.next_action_hint: Repair should not over-preserve scaffold cohorts after oracle placement; focus instead on pre-merge/fill ordering or safer template candidate choice.

description: Tried requiring dummy-free oracle templates when available

asi.hypothesis: Prefer fully occupied oracle templates over templates with dummy oracle people when both exist, reducing artificial contacts and projection ambiguity without case-specific logic.
asi.rollback_reason: The dummy-free filter caused one benchmark failure (transfer attribute sentinel placeholder) and worsened constrained SGP balance despite improving Sailing in this run.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not globally filter to dummy-free templates. Candidate selection needs feasibility/risk scoring that accounts for omitted people and constraints, not a hard dummy rule.

description: Tried prioritizing selected-region people before outside moves during oracle merge

asi.hypothesis: Accepted oracle targets should fill from the selected template region before pulling people from outside groups, reducing basin-disruptive outside moves while preserving coherent group-level oracle structure.
asi.rollback_reason: The result was near current best but did not improve the primary metric over the latest kept repeat; it also left several sentinels mixed, so the structural rule is not clearly robust.
asi.next_action_hint: Selected-region priority is promising but noisy; revisit only with stronger candidate-risk diagnostics or a fuller fill strategy.

description: Tried projecting only high-attendance people into oracle template

asi.hypothesis: The pure-contact oracle should be projected mainly onto high-attendance people so oracle-local identities represent stable participants rather than sparse attendees that create dummy-like holes across sessions.
asi.rollback_reason: High-attendance filtering improved some large/partial signals but worsened the primary metric, especially constrained SGP 49x7 and 169x13 sentinels.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not hard-filter low-attendance projectable people. Attendance should influence scoring, not eligibility, unless paired with candidate dimensions.

description: Tried skipping oracle when warmup scaffold has zero repeat penalty

asi.hypothesis: If the warmup scaffold already has zero raw repeat penalty and there is no maximize-unique-contacts objective, the repeat oracle has no repeat-score upside and should not disturb constraint/attribute structure.
asi.rollback_reason: The repeat-optimal scaffold guard was too broad and worsened the primary metric, likely skipping useful oracle geometry for search even when raw repeat penalty was zero at construction time.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep only the stricter total-score-zero guard. Repeat-component-only bypasses need more evidence and perhaps unique-contact/search-basin diagnostics.

description: Tried two-second warmup only when construction budget is at least four seconds

asi.hypothesis: The earlier 2s warmup improved relative mean but failed a small-budget constrained SGP case, so applying 2s only when construction budget is at least four seconds might capture scaffold-quality gains safely.
asi.rollback_reason: Budget-guarded 2s warmup removed the failure but worsened the primary metric and construction time, especially Sailing.
asi.next_action_hint: Longer warmup is not a simple win. Focus on merge/projection structure rather than spending more construction time globally.

description: Tried oracle-seeded baseline refill instead of direct scaffold overwrite merge

asi.hypothesis: Instead of destructively overwriting the scaffold, seed an otherwise empty schedule with frozen anchors plus projected oracle placements and let the legacy baseline fill the remaining movable slots to recover its search-friendly basin behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Oracle-seeded baseline refill did not improve the primary metric; it improved runtime and kept failures at zero but hurt synthetic/immovable sentinels and was weaker than the kept direct merge line.
asi.next_action_hint: A full oracle-guided fill needs objective-aware placement, not just baseline random fill after oracle seeding. Reuse of baseline alone is insufficient.

description: Tried scaffold fallback backfill for oracle template holes during merge

asi.hypothesis: When projected oracle groups have dummy/unmapped holes, fill remaining selected-region capacity with the original scaffold occupants before repair so the merge is less destructive without blocking oracle candidates.
asi.rollback_reason: The fallback backfill worsened the primary metric and constrained SGP 49x7 sentinel despite improving some individual cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Simple scaffold backfill can over-preserve local scaffold structure and weaken oracle geometry; future fill work needs objective-aware assignment rather than holes-only backfill.

description: Tried promoting lowest-risk candidate within top-three template frontier

asi.hypothesis: Within the leading estimated-score frontier, choose the structurally lowest-disruption oracle template to reduce basin damage without projection-score gating or case-specific logic.
asi.rollback_reason: Lowest-risk top-three promotion was much worse, particularly Sailing and constrained SGP 169x13, showing that the estimated-score ordering carries important contact-opportunity information that raw disruption risk cannot replace.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not select by disruption risk alone. Multi-template selection needs richer feasibility/contact tradeoff rather than a simple top-frontier risk promotion.
